quick_skim.title: When Agents Remember Too Much: Memory Poisoning Attacks on Large Language Model Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Personal AI agents powered by large language models can reason and act using available tools to access emails, manage calendars, and push code to remote repositories, all with minimal oversight. When augmented with long-term memory, an agent can recall specific details relevant to the current task, reducing the need for large context windows. Currently, long-term memory agents tend to fall into two distinct domains: conversational and action-planning agents. Personal assistant agents sit at the convergence of these two domains and handle sensitive information while interacting with untrusted information sources, creating previously unaccounted security vulnerabilities. In this work, we introduce the novel attack vector, GhostWriter, which exploits current memory subsystems in tool-using personal agents to poison their memory store. GhostWriter operates in two phases: injection, where an adversary sends a hidden attack payload to the target agent; and activation, in which the poisoned memory is retrieved. We show that GhostWriter achieves near-universal injection rates of approximately 98% and a high average activation rate of approximately 60% against state-of-the-art agents. This attack is possible due to the lack of security-focused memory governance. In response, we propose Agentic Memory Sentry (AM-Sentry), which leverages two mitigation techniques: a memory-saving policy and a memory-retrieval screen. Our experiments show that AM-Sentry dramatically reduces GhostWriter's success rate while preserving agent utility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: memory poisoning attacks on LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM智能体的记忆中毒攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM智能体的记忆中毒攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM智能体的记忆中毒攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GhostWriter attack exploits long-term memory in agent systems by injecting malicious content, revealing critical security vulnerabilities in memory management.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当LLM智能体配备长期记忆时，会面临来自不可信信息源的记忆中毒攻击。本文提出GhostWriter攻击向量，通过向记忆库注入恶意内容操控智能体行为。研究揭示了当前记忆管理策略中的安全漏洞，强调在记忆设计时需要同时考虑功能性与安全性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当LLM智能体配备长期记忆时，会面临来自不可信信息源的记忆中毒攻击。本文提出GhostWriter攻击向量，通过向记忆库注入恶意内容操控智能体行为。研究揭示了当前记忆管理策略中的安全漏洞，强调在记忆设计时需要同时考虑功能性与安全性。
